name: Sestil del Maíllo Stream and Mojonavalle Slope Trail
name_original: Chorrera de Mojonavalle – circular por los bosques de Canencia
description: Hike the moderate 7.1-mile Sestil del Maíllo Stream and Mojonavalle Slope Trail to see a waterfall and rare birch forest.
region_intro: Hiking around Canencia offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Norte of Madrid, situated in the upper Lozoya Valley. The region is characterized by surrounding mountains, extensive pine and oak forests, and unique natural features like the Birch Forest. Hikers can explore areas around the Puerto de Canencia, which serves as a starting point for many routes, and discover the Mojonavalle Waterfall. Best hiking trails around Canencia The most popular hiking route is Sestil del Maillo and the slope of Mojonavalle — circular, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied terrain near the Mojonavalle area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sestil del Maíllo Stream and Mojonavalle Slope Trail, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This trail follows the Sestil del Maíllo Stream and ascends the Mojonavalle slope, offering scenic views. Local hikers also love the Mojonavalle Forest – Canencia Pass loop from Puerto de Canencia, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through pine forests and past the Canencia Pass, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Canencia is defined by its mountain landscapes, unique birch forests, and extensive pine and oak woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging paths. The routes in Canencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 240 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Canencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Canencia?
A: Canencia offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging paths through the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte of Madrid.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Canencia?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Canencia&lt;/a&gt; and its surrounding forests are particularly stunning in autumn when the birch and oak leaves change color. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery, especially after rainfall when the waterfalls are at their best.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes in Canencia?
A: Canencia's hiking trails traverse diverse landscapes, including extensive Scots pine and oak forests, unique birch woodlands, and mountainous terrain. You'll encounter streams, open areas with panoramic views, and the distinct microclimate of the Birch Forest.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hiking trails in Canencia?
A: Yes, a notable natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de Mojonavalle&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful 30-meter high waterfall. It's best visited after heavy rains or snowmelt for a more spectacular flow and is accessible via forest tracks from the Canencia Pass.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking options in Canencia?
A: Yes, Canencia offers some easier routes suitable for families. For instance, an 'Ecological Path' with a gentle elevation gain is available, typically lasting about 2 hours and covering approximately 6.5 km, allowing families to enjoy nature together.

Q: Are there dog-friendly trails in Canencia?
A: Many trails in Canencia are suitable for hiking with dogs, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. It's always advisable to check local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash when necessary.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Canencia?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Canencia are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229377718/sestil-del-maillo-and-the-slope-of-mojonavalle-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sestil del Maillo and the slope of Mojonavalle — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate loop through varied terrain.

Q: What unique natural features can I explore in Canencia?
A: Canencia is home to the rare Birch Forest, a unique microclimate supporting birch, holly, and Scots pines. Within this forest, you can find the remarkable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centennial Yew of Canencia&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient tree estimated to be 400-500 years old.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, Canencia features three medieval bridges of historical interest: Puente Canto, Puente Cadenas, and Puente Matafrailes. There is a dedicated route that links all three, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views in Canencia?
A: For splendid panoramic views of the Canencia Valley, head to the Mirador de la Chorrera. It's an excellent spot for hikers to pause and appreciate the expansive scenery.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Canencia?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Canencia&lt;/a&gt;, located about 9 kilometers from the town, features a recreational area with ample parking, picnic spots, and fountains, making it a convenient starting point for many routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Canencia?
A: The trails in Canencia are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 240 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the forests, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Canencia?
A: Canencia offers a range of difficulty levels. While there are a few easy routes, the majority of the 43 available trails are considered moderate, with a smaller number of more challenging options for experienced hikers.
tips: One of the many chorreras that the Community of Madrid offers, this one in particular is very easily accessible and its surroundings surrounded by birch trees make it worth visiting. If you visit in winter you may find it frozen, a nice postcard.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts from the well-equipped recreational area of Puerto de Canencia. You'll find ample parking available there, making it an easy starting point for your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Sestil del Maíllo Stream and Mojonavalle Slope Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Sierra de Guadarrama region, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery year-round. It's particularly stunning in autumn when the foliage changes color, and in winter, the frozen Mojonavalle Runoff can be a spectacular sight. Spring and summer also provide refreshing walks through the diverse forests.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sestil del Maíllo Stream and Mojonavalle Slope Trail, nor for general access to this part of the Sierra de Guadarrama. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: The main circular loop typically takes between 2 to 2.5 hours to complete. If you opt for an extended version of the route, it could take up to 4.5 hours.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered to be of medium-low difficulty, making it suitable for most fitness levels, including families and those new to hiking. The elevation changes are moderate, contributing to its accessibility.

faqs.question: What natural features or points of interest can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the impressive Mojonavalle Runoff (Chorrera de Mojonavalle) waterfall, which drops over 50 meters. The trail also passes through the rare 'abedular de Canencia' (birch forest) and by the ancient Centennial Yew of Puerto de Canencia. The Mirador de la Chorrera de Mojonavalle offers excellent views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of dirt paths. You'll start on a forest track through a dense Scots pine forest, transitioning to narrower paths ('trochas'). There are gentle descents along the slope, and some sections feature a 'pétreo acceso' (stony access) near the waterfall.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The trail boasts rich biodiversity. You'll walk through forests of Scots pine, birch, yew, holly, willow, dog roses, and heather. Unusually, you might also spot specimens of Douglas fir. The area is home to various wildlife, though sightings can depend on the time of day and season.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Senda del Arroyo del Sestil del Maíllo y de la Ladera de Mojonavalle, Pinares de Canencia, and GR 10.1.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts at the well-equipped recreational area of Puerto de Canencia, where you'll find facilities. However, there are no specific facilities or potable water sources directly along the hiking route itself, so it's advisable to carry enough water for your journey.

waypoints.name: Centennial Yew of Puerto de Canencia
